subject: Public Health
title: Water Treatment Methods
description: A 60-second lesson on Water Treatment Methods.

hook.text: Chlorination—adding tiny amounts of chlorine to water—may be the greatest public health achievement ever.

buildup.text: Filtration, boiling, UV treatment, and chemical disinfection all make water safe to drink.

discovery.text: Municipal water treatment plants use multiple barriers—coagulation, sedimentation, filtration, then disinfection.

twist.text: Simple household filters—like ceramic or biosand filters—work remarkably well in areas without infrastructure.

climax.text: Point-of-use water treatment saves lives immediately while communities wait for piped water infrastructure.

punchline.text: Making water safe doesn't require high technology. It requires commitment.

quiz.question: What is considered one of the greatest public health achievements?
